rationale: 50 consecutive Event ID 4625 (failed logon) events were generated within approximately 181 milliseconds on host-01.corp.local, all originating from the same non-standard process (C:\Tools\[x]\[x].exe) under subject user-01 using the legacy MSV1_0 auth package (A0, EV-001 through EV-049). Every target username is a unique, random-looking 8-character uppercase string (e.g., VFRKKMDG, AACECLCL, RAFNYQZG), consistent with automated username enumeration or credential stuffing against the local logon subsystem via the LogonUser API — not a human typing mistakes. Sub-status 0xc0000064 ('user does not exist') across all events confirms the target accounts are non-existent, strongly indicating account enumeration rather than password spraying against known accounts. The originating process path (C:\Tools\[x]\[x].exe) is non-standard, unidentified, and not a recognized system or vendor binary, warranting immediate investigation to identify and contain the tool.
